109797.explanation: We want diversity in the board, but quality comes first. A mandatory 50/50 split (equal men and women) is not necessary. We look for the best people and strive for a good mix of men and women in the executive board.

109798.explanation: We like to listen to residents, but prefer dialogue and direct contact over referenda. We engage with the population before decisions are made.

109801.explanation: We support enterprising farmers, but "always allowing" goes too far. A farm shop or mini-campsite is often possible, but it must fit the environment. We assess each situation in good consultation with the surroundings.

109802.explanation: We are for sustainable energy, but the municipality does not need to provide extra funding. Residents who generate energy together can use existing subsidies. This way, costs remain affordable for everyone.

109803.explanation: We do not want uncontrolled proliferation of large distribution centers in Beek. We choose to maintain livability and prefer to support local entrepreneurs.

109805.explanation: Seasonal workers deserve decent housing. Employers in agriculture and horticulture must take responsibility and arrange accommodation on their own premises. This prevents nuisance and keeps the housing market available for residents.

109809.explanation: If it turns out there is a lot of interest, we will facilitate this. We should not have the illusion that this will prevent the purchase and setting off of illegal fireworks.

109810.explanation: Free bulky waste collection sounds good, but then the costs fall on all residents. We want to keep the burden fair for everyone. We find the current drop-off system (where you contribute for your own bulky waste) reasonable to cover costs.

109814.explanation: A strong local broadcaster connects the community. We find it important that our local broadcaster can continue to exist to provide independent local news and information. Should extra municipal support be needed, we will consider that.

109816.explanation: Making music is valuable for development. Every child should have the opportunity to learn an instrument. We want the municipality to subsidize music lessons at primary schools or through clubs. This way, our youth grow up with culture and community spirit.

109818.explanation: Our heritage is valuable to the community. We do not want to cut funding for monument care. Traditions and historic sites give Beek its character. We will continue to invest in them to preserve them for future generations. In fact, we want to explore whether we can upgrade some monuments.

109819.explanation: Safety is paramount. We want more community enforcement officers (BOAs) on the streets as the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. With extra enforcers, we can better tackle disturbances and ensure residents feel safe in their area.

109820.explanation: More camera surveillance can help prevent nuisance and crime. We support additional cameras in places where needed. This increases the feeling of safety and assists the police and BOAs in catching offenders.

109826.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits can give something back to society. We believe a reciprocal obligation is fair. Socially useful work keeps people active and involved, builds self-confidence, and helps the community. Tailored solutions remain important.

109828.explanation: Aid for poverty should go to those who truly need it. We will not raise the income threshold. Otherwise, people with somewhat higher incomes would also receive help. We focus on supporting the lowest incomes (the minima).

109831.explanation: We support traffic safety, but not every street needs to be limited to 30 km/h by default. Main roads can remain at 50 km/h with safe crossings and speed bumps where necessary. A general reduction to 30 km/h on all streets goes too far for us.

109832.explanation: People must feel comfortable with their care. Everyone should be able to choose a care provider that matches their faith, culture, or beliefs. Freedom of choice in healthcare is paramount and always possible through a personal budget.

109838.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce, so we prefer not to give automatic priority to status holders (recognized refugees). First, we want to house our own residents. We also help refugees with status but not at the expense of long waiting times for Beek residents. However, we must meet our quotas.

109839.explanation: We want waste to be disposed of properly. Providing free access to the recycling center lowers barriers and prevents dumping in nature. We make dropping off bulky waste and other trash as free as possible so everyone can dispose of it properly.

109840.explanation: Sustainable energy is important, but a total ban on solar panels on meadows goes too far. In some cases, solar parks on agricultural land can be acceptable if well integrated. We weigh nature, agriculture, and energy needs carefully on a case-by-case basis. Good alternatives exist, such as solar poles in or on g

109845.explanation: Affordable housing is crucial. We want at least 30% of new homes in Beek to be social rental. This gives people with lower incomes a better chance of finding a home. We build for all groups, not just high incomes.

109848.explanation: Every community deserves a place to come together. We want a village or community center in all villages. Meeting each other strengthens the community. This way, we bring people together and keep every area livable and active.

109849.explanation: Parking pressure in the center is high. We want more parking spaces in the heart of Beek. This keeps the center accessible and attractive for shopkeepers and visitors. Extra parking reduces traffic searching for a spot and makes things easier for everyone.

109853.explanation: Speed bumps are not always the best solution. For these streets, we prefer other measures against speeding (such as effective enforcement or smart adjustments). Bumps can cause nuisance, so we install them only when absolutely necessary.

109855.explanation: Elderly people have a lot of knowledge and experience. We therefore want a senior advisory council. This gives seniors a clear voice in policy. We listen to their ideas so plans better align with their needs.

109797.explanation: Quality, experience, and commitment to Beek should be leading, not gender. A council should be a good reflection based on competencies and trust, so residents get the best administrators.

109798.explanation: Beek Lokaal chooses direct involvement through participation, consultation, and dialogue. Referenda oversimplify complex decisions, while tailored approaches, dialogue, and shared decision-making better fit an involved and reliable local government.

109801.explanation: Side activities such as a shop or campsite strengthen farmers' income models and keep rural areas vital. Provided they fit the environment and consider nature and livability, the municipality should facilitate this.

109802.explanation: Local energy initiatives by residents deserve support. They increase support for sustainability, lower energy costs, and strengthen the community. The municipality should facilitate this, if feasible and affordable.

109803.explanation: On sites like Aviation Valley, there is room for carefully integrated, sustainable logistics. New distribution centers can create jobs for Beek residents, provided they suit the location, infrastructure, and livability.

109805.explanation: Beek Lokaal wants to support agricultural entrepreneurs, not impose extra obligations. Housing for seasonal workers requires cooperation between the sector, municipality, and region to ensure it is safe, affordable, and well organized.

109809.explanation: A municipal fireworks show provides a festive and safe New Year's Eve for everyone. It limits nuisance and risks, brings people together, and fits a responsible, collective alternative to consumer fireworks.

109810.explanation: Free bulky waste collection increases costs for all residents. Beek Lokaal opts for an accessible recycling center and good waste separation to keep it affordable and sustainable.

109814.explanation: The municipality supports the local broadcaster within its legal task until 2028. Additional funding is only possible if there is demonstrable added value, sufficient support, and room in the budget. Beek Lokaal chooses realism and cooperation.

109816.explanation: Music lessons promote cognitive, social, and creative development. With municipal subsidies, every primary school child can participate regardless of financial situation. This aligns with Beek Lokaal's vision on equal opportunities and cultural participation.

109818.explanation: Monuments are part of our identity. Beek Lokaal wants to protect heritage and implement cultural policy. Cutting monument care harms history and livability. We choose preservation over demolition.

109819.explanation: Safety is a basic need. Beek Lokaal wants residents to feel safe and chooses sufficient supervision. More BOAs help with enforcement and prevention so our communities remain clean, livable, and safe.

109820.explanation: Illegal dumping in rural areas must be tackled firmly. It damages nature, safety, and livability. Targeted enforcement, camera surveillance at hotspots, and cooperation with residents help effectively combat this.

109826.explanation: Participation is important, but mandatory community work does not work for everyone. Beek Lokaal opts for tailored support, guidance, and activation where possible, considering personal circumstances and talents.

109828.explanation: Raising the income threshold also helps working poor and families just above the norm. This prevents people from falling through the cracks and ensures more residents receive timely support.

109831.explanation: Beek Lokaal wants to improve traffic safety but not a blanket 30 km/h limit everywhere. We support implementing 30 km zones where safety improves, such as near school routes and dangerous spots. A general reduction throughout built-up areas is not our starting point.

109832.explanation: Freedom of choice in care is a legal right in the Netherlands according to the Health Insurance Act and Long-Term Care Act. Beek Lokaal wants inclusive care and tailored support. Free choice of a care provider that fits faith, culture, or beliefs strengthens trust and quality of care.

109838.explanation: Status holders have a right to housing, but priority can be perceived as unfair by other home seekers. A balanced approach is needed: meet legal obligations and ensure equal opportunities through temporary solutions and turnover.

109839.explanation: Free disposal prevents dumping and encourages recycling but incurs municipal costs. Many waste streams are already free. A balanced approach: free for reusable waste and during special action days, low fees for residual waste.

109840.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can contribute to the energy transition and provide extra income for farmers. The municipality must apply tailored solutions: carefully integrated, with attention to landscape, nature, and food production.

109845.explanation: The 30-30-40 distribution creates balance: social rental, affordable home ownership, and private sector. This provides space for starters, families, and seniors and keeps building aligned with Beek’s needs, per community and with tailored solutions.

109848.explanation: Beek Lokaal wants to maintain meeting places and basic facilities in all communities but does not impose an obligation to create a full village center in every area. Our focus is on preserving livability and strengthening social connections.

109849.explanation: Beek Lokaal wants to maintain sufficient free parking to ensure accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs. When reconstructing the Market, the emphasis is on greening and quality of stay, but retaining parking spaces remains a priority.

109853.explanation: Beek Lokaal wants to improve traffic safety and address dangerous spots in all communities, including these streets. Measures such as 30 km zones and physical interventions (e.g., speed bumps) can enhance safety.

109855.explanation: A senior advisory council can cooperate with the youth advisory council (Veer zeen jongk!) and the Social Domain Advisory Council. By combining knowledge and experiences, a broadly supported policy emerges in which all generations contribute ideas on inclusion, care, and participation.

109797.explanation: As far as possible, we strive for a good balance of men and women in the council.

109798.explanation: Democracy starts with trust in people. We want to give people more influence on the decisions that affect them. Not just every four years during elections, but continuously. A referendum fits into this.

109801.explanation: If these activities serve a subordinate function and are carried out alongside the main activity, then that should be allowed.

109802.explanation: We support initiatives related to generating and saving energy that originate from streets, neighborhoods, and cooperatives. If needed, a financial contribution in the form of a loan from the municipality should be possible.

109803.explanation: As long as this fits within the business park and zoning regulations, new distribution centers may be established.

109805.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture must ensure proper housing for seasonal workers. This does not have to be on their own business premises.

109809.explanation: With the introduction of a national fireworks ban, private individuals may no longer set off fireworks. We see a role for associations to create something beautiful with municipal support. The role of the municipality is to facilitate and subsidize where necessary.

109810.explanation: If bulky waste is collected free of charge at home, local taxes will rise. Most people are able to bring their bulky waste to a recycling center themselves. The social network can additionally help those who are unable to do so.

109814.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for sharing local news with residents. Municipalities are legally required to finance their local broadcaster. With this money, the broadcaster provides an adequate local media offering.

109816.explanation: Art and culture enrich life. We pay attention to this within the municipality as well. Every primary school child should have the opportunity to develop themselves culturally.

109818.explanation: Monuments are part of the cultural heritage of the municipality. It is not appropriate to cut back on this.

109819.explanation: In the current council term, the number of BOAs (special enforcement officers) has been increased. The municipality now has enough BOAs dealing with safety.

109820.explanation: Camera surveillance is a last resort when other means to maintain public order are insufficient. Only in very specific problems may (temporary) camera surveillance be considered.

109826.explanation: Meaningful social work is part of the Participation Act. This must not obstruct regular reintegration. Each person is assessed individually to determine what is appropriate, aiming to encourage participation in society.

109828.explanation: The Municipality of Beek must raise the income threshold from 120% to 150% of the social assistance norm. This will equalize the level within the Western Mining Region. For help and support, it should not matter where you live.

109831.explanation: By lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h in built-up areas, roads become safer and the chance of accidents decreases. The built-up area thus becomes safer for pedestrians and cyclists, as well as for children playing outside.

109832.explanation: Under the Social Support Act (WMO), freedom of choice is legally established. It is an important right that allows people to choose their care provider without municipal interference.

109838.explanation: The national government determines every six months how many status holders a municipality must accommodate. The municipality must adhere to this task. The manner in which the statutory task is fulfilled does not matter.

109839.explanation: The volume of waste streams, especially residual waste, must be reduced. Charging a fee for disposing of residual waste encourages reduction of this waste stream. Additionally, we want to maintain the current free waste streams such as paper, PMD, bulky garden waste, and glass.

109840.explanation: Beek is a pleasant municipality to live in, now and in the future. That is why we must invest now in measures to counter climate change. Solar panels help with the transition to sustainable energy. This is also possible on agricultural land.

109845.explanation: People with lower or middle incomes must be able to find good quality housing. There is a large shortage of social rental housing, and many must be newly built to meet demand, also in Beek.

109848.explanation: A village center or community house is a place where people from a neighborhood can come together. These places foster connection within the neighborhood and help combat loneliness. They are therefore very important for every neighborhood.

109849.explanation: There is sufficient parking in and around the town center. By cycling more often, we can prevent traffic congestion and encourage active movement. We want to realize more bicycle parking spaces.

109853.explanation: Traffic calming measures have already been taken in these streets. Additional measures are not necessary here. Other streets in the municipality are more suitable candidates.

109855.explanation: Young people have few other ways to make their voice heard in Beek. Older people do have these means. Therefore, there is no need for a specific advisory council.

109797.explanation: That there are currently only male aldermen, despite several changes during the term, is a missed opportunity. At the same time, the best candidate must always be appointed to the council, regardless of gender.

109798.explanation: Referendums can contribute to better-supported decisions on major projects. The discussion about the new school and the reconstruction of Schimmerterweg without a segregated cycle path shows that important choices sometimes do not sufficiently reflect the opinion of many Beek residents.

109801.explanation: A small-scale shop clearly subordinate to the agricultural business is basically not a problem as long as immediate neighbors have no legitimate objections. However, activities like a campsite justifiably require additional rules, including fire safety, waste, noise, and traffic.

109802.explanation: Citizen initiatives for sustainable energy (e.g., joint solar panels) must be actively stimulated by the municipality. Financial support (subsidy, loan, guarantee) is a logical instrument, provided it is well regulated and accessible to everyone.

109803.explanation: Distribution centers should not be generically opposed, especially not on designated locations such as Aviation Valley near MAA. Additional businesses can contribute to employment and municipal revenues, provided the effects on traffic, environment, and sustainability are demonstrably manageable.

109805.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture are primarily responsible for housing their seasonal workers themselves. Housing on or directly near their own premises prevents extra pressure on the housing market and nuisance in residential areas, provided applicable regulations are met and municipal supervision is maintained.

109809.explanation: Now that private fireworks are prohibited, a fireworks display organized by the municipality can contribute to a safe and shared New Year's Eve. This way, residents retain a festive moment while safety, supervision, and control are better guaranteed.

109810.explanation: In other countries, bulky waste is collected free of charge at home, which leads to less illegal dumping. This prevents additional cleaning and enforcement costs for the municipality and contributes to a cleaner living environment.

109814.explanation: The municipality can financially support the local broadcaster, provided it operates independently, is transparent about spending, and demonstrably contributes to good information provision for all residents.

109816.explanation: Music education contributes to children's development and equal opportunities. Until now, much municipal money has gone to sports; the Interest of Beek party finds it important that culture, such as music lessons, is also structurally supported.

109818.explanation: Monument care is important for preserving local heritage and identity. At the same time, public funds must be handled carefully. Budget cuts should not lead to deferred maintenance or loss of valuable heritage, but efficiency and prioritization are possible.

109819.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and livability. Additional BOAs can ensure better compliance and quicker action against nuisance. At known problem locations, supplementary camera surveillance can be used, with clear frameworks and respect for privacy, to support enforcement.

109820.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and prevent nuisance. In Beek municipality, it must be targeted at known problem locations, temporary and proportional, with clear guarantees for privacy and monitoring of use.

109826.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits are expected, where possible, to perform meaningful social work. This contributes to participation and self-esteem. Personal circumstances, health, and capabilities must always be taken into account.

109828.explanation: More and more residents with low or middle incomes struggle to make ends meet. By raising the income threshold for support, the municipality of Beek can also help this group in time and prevent financial problems from escalating.

109831.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h within built-up areas is not desirable everywhere. On residential streets and near schools, 30 km/h can contribute to safety and livability, but on main access roads, tailored solutions must remain possible in the municipality of Beek.

109832.explanation: The quality and availability of healthcare must always be paramount. Where possible, religious beliefs, culture, or convictions may be considered, but this must never come at the expense of good, accessible, and timely care.

109838.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit have the right to housing, but priority for social rental homes can no longer be justified while many residents of the municipality of Beek wait for years for a home. Belang van Beek believes that local residents should receive priority.

109839.explanation: Free disposal of waste at the recycling center lowers the threshold for proper waste separation and can prevent illegal dumping. This contributes to a clean living environment and can ultimately save costs for the municipality of Beek.

109840.explanation: Agricultural land remains primarily for agriculture; solar panels are allowed only exceptionally and under strict conditions.

109845.explanation: With about 28% social housing nationally, tailored solutions are necessary. In Beek, each project must consider the right mix, rather than applying a generic 35% standard.

109848.explanation: Village centers are important for social interaction and livability, but having a center in every village is not always feasible. In the municipality of Beek, each village should be assessed individually to determine what is needed and appropriate, including using existing facilities and promoting cooperation between villages.

109849.explanation: Additional parking capacity in the center of Beek is necessary for accessibility and livability. Belang van Beek sees opportunities for a multi-level parking facility behind the town hall and partially covering the Keutelbeek with grates, allowing parking to be possible again.

109853.explanation: Traffic measures such as speed bumps can contribute to traffic safety. Accessibility for ambulance and fire services must always be considered. Where necessary, alternatives such as raised platforms, road narrowing, or smart speed reducers can be applied.

109855.explanation: Seniors are already represented in the council. Belang van Beek actively participates in the children's council and values this form of youth participation. We do not consider an additional advisory council for seniors necessary.

109798.explanation: Good preliminary consultation with stakeholders. Listening well. Taking a responsible position and ultimately the council has the final say.

109801.explanation: Always must is too far. Where asked to undertake such an activity, it is about "thinking along with the applicant" and not saying "no" in advance.

109802.explanation: First through national and provincial schemes. The municipality should wisely facilitate. This could possibly also lead to financial contributions.

109803.explanation: The municipality of Beek has industrial estates for that purpose. These are the locations where it is perfectly feasible.

109805.explanation: This is not an issue of this scale in the municipality of Beek.

109809.explanation: That could be an option. Also consider whether it can be done regionally.

109810.explanation: Nothing is free. The costs are included in the waste disposal tax.

109814.explanation: The current scheme has just started. Let's wait and see how it goes first.

109816.explanation: Music lessons. That's what it's about. Can also be through associations. These then receive subsidies.

109818.explanation: The municipality must safeguard cultural heritage.

109819.explanation: Not needed at the moment. If temporarily more capacity is required, "borrowing from neighbors".

109828.explanation: There is already a reasonable system. Coordinate regionally.

109831.explanation: Main thoroughfares within built-up areas max 50 km/h. Others can be 30 km/h.

109832.explanation: The municipality does not have a decisive role in that.

109839.explanation: Nothing is free. Costs are included in the waste tax.

109849.explanation: There is no room for more than there already is.

109853.explanation: It is a through road with also a lot of truck and agricultural traffic, so speed bumps are out of the question.

109855.explanation: There are enough opportunities to raise awareness of and take into account this specific target group.

statements.title: Speed bumps should be installed in Raadhuisstraat, Brugstraat and Adsteeg.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The residents of Beek should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Beek moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Beek should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
At least 35% of the newly built homes in Beek should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Beek moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Beek should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the center of Beek.
Beek should get an advisory council for the elderly. The municipality already has a youth advisory council.
